commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=ac5626298f59b0ede59de4979d417b9eecec16b7)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a heuristic lightweight auto-reply lane for low-risk text-only replies and a 5-minute Codex initial-progress watchdog for accepted-but-silent turns.

PR surface: Source +260, Tests +255. Total +515 across 7 files.

Reproducibility: yes. for the blocking compatibility issue from source: an agent-level skill filter can be resolved into `mergedSkillFilter` while the new lightweight admission only sees raw `opts`, then disables tools. No high-confidence live reproduction was supplied for the PR's claimed behavior changes.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] Maintainer review and contributor action are needed for the configured-skill compatibility bug, real behavior proof, timeout product tradeoff, and branch conflicts; this is not a safe autonomous repair lane.

target.title: feat(auto-reply,codex): lightweight reply lane and Codex watchdog
target.body:
## Summary
- add a conservative lightweight reply lane for obvious low-risk text-only chat replies
- keep uncertain, tool/action, media/link, high-stakes, code/config, and unresolved reply-target messages on the full path
- add a Codex initial-progress watchdog that caps the accepted-but-silent gap at 5 minutes, clamped to the configured attempt timeout, and restores the full timeout once item-level progress arrives

## Why
- normal conversational replies should not pay the full setup/tool wiring cost when no tools are needed
- over-escalation remains harmless because borderline messages stay on the full agent path
- Codex runs that are accepted but silent should not wait the entire attempt timeout before surfacing as stalled

## Real behavior proof
- Behavior or issue addressed: OpenClaw Telegram replies now have a conservative lightweight admission lane for obvious low-risk text-only chat, and Codex accepted-but-silent turns have a short initial-progress watchdog instead of waiting the whole attempt timeout.
- Real environment tested: Moeed Ahmed's real Mac Mini OpenClaw installation, installed package path under `/opt/homebrew/lib/node_modules/openclaw`, Telegram Operator Governance topic, OpenClaw `2026.6.6`.
- Exact steps or command run after the patch:
  ```bash
  rg -n "resolve_lightweight_lane|CODEX_TURN_INITIAL_PROGRESS_IDLE_TIMEOUT_MS|isLowRiskTelegramReply" /opt/homebrew/lib/node_modules/openclaw -g "*.js" -g "*.mjs"
  openclaw doctor --non-interactive
  ```
- Evidence after fix:
  Terminal output from the real installed runtime included:
  ```text
  /opt/homebrew/lib/node_modules/openclaw/dist/run-attempt-BtRhrKv-.js:90:const CODEX_TURN_INITIAL_PROGRESS_IDLE_TIMEOUT_MS = 5 * 6e4;
  /opt/homebrew/lib/node_modules/openclaw/dist/run-attempt-BtRhrKv-.js:1139:...notification.method === "turn/started" ? { attemptTimeoutMs: CODEX_TURN_INITIAL_PROGRESS_IDLE_TIMEOUT_MS } : {}
  /opt/homebrew/lib/node_modules/openclaw/dist/get-reply-DW1jVSLI.js:5149:const lightweightOpts = resolverTiming.measureSync("reply.resolve_lightweight_lane", () => applyLightweightReplyLane(opts, {
  Doctor complete.
  ```
- Observed result after fix: The installed runtime contained both patch points, the gateway stayed usable after restart, and Telegram Operator Governance follow-up messages continued through the real OpenClaw setup. Doctor completed with unrelated existing hygiene warnings only.
- What was not tested: No additional gap for this PR proof. The PR itself remains draft until GitHub checks and maintainer review are complete.

## Tests
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/lightweight-lane.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/attempt-notification-state.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`
target.author: moeedahmed
